description: Body Shop management — enforces aftermarket parts disclosure, OEM repair procedures, anti-steering laws, environmental compliance, OSHA PPE requirements, and monitors DRP performance

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any body shop operation
AI-generated repair estimates and recommendations must be reviewed by a certified collision technician
repair estimate, supplement, and parts data must be consistent — no mismatched line items or duplicate charges
state law requires disclosure when aftermarket (non-OEM) parts are used in a collision repair — customer must be informed and consent
OEM repair procedures must be followed for structural and safety-related repairs — deviation creates liability for the shop and dealership
NEVER steer insurance customers to or from a specific shop — anti-steering laws prohibit this practice in most states
paint booth emissions, solvent disposal, and hazardous waste handling must meet EPA and state environmental regulations
OSHA PPE requirements must be met for all body shop operations — respiratory protection, eye protection, hearing protection as applicable
all structural repairs must be fully documented with pre- and post-repair measurements — required for safety certification

soft_flags.logic: repair cycle time is exceeding the DRP (direct repair program) target set by the insurance partner
supplement request denial rate from insurers exceeds the acceptable threshold
customer satisfaction index is below the DRP program's required threshold — risking program removal
sublet work turnaround time is exceeding the expected timeline — delaying overall repair completion
